summary:
Thioamides commonly cause liver damage and low white blood cell counts but rarely lead to life-threatening allergies; thus, anaphylaxis does not fit this category for these medications' primary concerns.

Key Points Addressed:
- Common serious adverse events related to thiamide use include hepatic impairment & leukopenia/AGN.
- Life threatening allergy/anaphylactic reaction less common compared to direct organ/systemic impacts. - Life threatening allergy/anaphylactic reaction less common compared to direct organ/systemic impacts.
